Year: 2013
Volume: 3
Issue: 4
Nutritional Status, Physical Activity and Energy Balance among Rural Adolescent Girls of Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh
Author:
A.B. Srilatha1,, A.B. Subhashini2, D. Baby3
Total Page Count: 4
DOI:
Page Number: 388 to 391

1Department of Home Science, S.V. University, Tirupati

2Department of Anthropology, S.V. University, Tirupati

3Department of Home Science, S.V. University, Tirupati

*Corresponding author email id: srilathaamudala@gmail.com

Online published on 3 February, 2014.

Abstract

The present investigation is to assess the nutritional status of adolescent girls around 13 to 18 years age from 3 different mandals of Ramachandrapuram, Renigunta and Chandragiri, constituted the sample of the study. Total sample size is 200 derived in two age groups of 100 in each group. First is 13 to 15 years age girls and the second is 16 to 18 years age girls. Nutritional status assessed by both the groups compared with their Body Mass Index (BMI), Mid Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) compared with ICMR and NCHS standards are low in 13 to 15 years group age. Dietary intakes are very low imbalanced in 13 to 15 years group, 16–18 years are better (60%) as compared to Recommended Dietary Allowance (RDA). There is a significant difference between the energy intake and expenditure in both the age group of girls. There is a negative energy balance in both the age groups. Energy is needed for both the age groups for performing household and farm activities to safeguard their peak growth. There is a chronic energy deficiency observed in the present study. Hence, there is a need for suitable intervention and nutrition education and nutritional counselling to prevent under nutrition in future.

Keywords

Nutritional status, BMI, MUAC, RDA, Growth
